title: Crispy Indian Peanuts Curry
description: A dense, salty Andhra-inspired curry packed with roasted peanuts, perfect for a post-workout meal.
instructions: Preheat oven to 200°C (392°F). Spread peanuts on a baking sheet and roast for 8 minutes, turning halfway until lightly browned and fragrant. Set aside.
In a deep pan, heat coconut oil over medium heat. Add mustard and cumin seeds; when they begin to pop, add the chopped onions.
Cook onions, stirring frequently, until golden brown, about 5-7 minutes.
Add minced garlic and grated ginger; sauté for another minute until aromatic.
Stir in tomato puree, turmeric, and coriander powder. Cook, stirring, until the mixture thickens and the oil separates, about 5 minutes.
Add the roasted peanuts and a splash of water (≈50 ml) to reach desired thickness. Season with salt to taste.
Simmer the curry gently for 8 minutes, allowing flavors to meld and the peanuts to absorb the spices.
Stir in the chopped fresh coriander leaves, reserving a few for garnish.
(Optional) Transfer the hot curry into a preheated tandoor or an oven set to 240°C. Roast for 2 minutes to crisp the tops, or simply sprinkle a drizzle of ghee for extra crunch.
Serve hot with steamed rice or flatbread, garnished with remaining coriander.
